finding: Between Apr 10-14, there were 118 human sessions across 5 consecutive days (avg 24/day), with peaks of 35 sessions on both Apr 11 and Apr 12, spanning 08:45 to 23:37. This is a manic-mode signature: sustained high-intensity bursts across multiple days without a visible rest day. The user's stated wellbeing goals include 'mindful forest walks, breaks from screen' and mixing productive output with being present and mindful. Five consecutive marathon days works against that.
existing_mitigation: late-night-warn.py hook exists but only triggers per-session after 20:00 with 5+ active hours. It has no concept of multi-day streaks or cumulative fatigue, so it cannot catch this pattern.

finding: Human sessions past 22:00 appeared on 12 of 18 active days in the last 30 days. On several nights the activity extended well past 23:00: Apr 7 (23:26-23:54 then 00:00-00:01), Apr 10 (22:12-23:28, six sessions), Apr 11 (22:00-23:37, eleven sessions in 97 minutes), and Apr 14-15 (22:18 through 00:41). The late-night-warn.py hook is present and would fire after 20:00, but its 60-minute cooldown means it warns once and then goes silent for the remainder of the cluster. The frequency of post-23:00 work suggests the nudge is either not strong enough or being overridden.
existing_mitigation: late-night-warn.py fires after 20:00 with active-hour thresholds. It does nudge, but the 60-minute cooldown and soft tone may be insufficient given that late-night clusters recur on most active days.
